rulings.Case Title: LOUIS NEFF VS. POPE & POPE PROPERTIES, LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 8, 2011, line 6, DEFENDANTS POPE & POPE PROPERTIES, LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED, JAMES POPE DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 1st cause of action is overruled. Whether the Note was an integrated document is not an issue that should be decided on demurrer. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 2nd and 3rd causes of action is overruled. Plaintiff¿s allegation that Defendant did not intend to repay the loan within 6 months would not have accrued until 10/4/07. Therefore the fraud cause of action is timely on this ground which is enough to save the entire cause of action on demurrer. Defendant¿s Demurrer on the 5th cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead the time and manner of discovery and the inability to have made earlier discovery despite reasonable diligence. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 4th cause of action is overruled. Plaintiff has alleged sufficient facts to plead around the Statute of Limitations at eh demurrer stage. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 7th and 8th causes of action is overruled. Although the Court agrees that plaintiff¿s pursuit of these causes of action and onerous discovery despite Defendant¿s stipulation is on the verge of oppressive litigation and a waste of attorney and court resources (which the Court frowns upon), there is nothing that legally precludes his pursuing these claims. The Court is ordering the parties to a settlement conference within the next 30 days. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: JAMIE HERNANDEZ et al VS. MARTIN RESENDEZ GUAJARDO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 8, 2011, line 9, PLAINTIFFS JAMIE HERNANDEZ, JAGDEEP SINGH, GUILLERMO ORTEGA DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. As to the 2nd, 3rd, 4th, 5th, 13th, 16th, 19th, 20th, 21st, 23rd, 24th, 25th, 26th, 27th, 28th, 29th 30th, 31st, 33rd, 35th, 37th and 38th Defenses, the Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. These do not constitute valid defenses and/or are more appropriately addressed through other pleadings, motions or oppositions to motions. As to the 7th, 8th, 10th, 11th, 12th, 14th, 15th, 17th, 18th, 22nd, and 34th defenses, the Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend. Defendant must plead facts, and where applicable, specific statutory references or privileges to support the defenses. As to the 6th defense the Demurrer is overruled. =(302/LMG)
